$comment: Copy to config/telegram.json and fill in. That file is gitignored.
Get your numeric Telegram ID from @userinfobot. IDs are used rather than
@usernames because usernames can be changed and reused; IDs cannot.
The bot REFUSES TO START with an empty allowedUserIds list. An open
custodial bot would let strangers create wallets on your machine.
IMPORTANT: this is a custodial setup. Your machine holds the private keys
for every user listed here. Only invite people who understand that, and
keep defaultCaps low enough that a total loss would be survivable for them.
Hard ceilings per user. Cannot be raised from Telegram - only here.
maxBridgeUsdc MUST be greater than maxSpendUsdc: USDC is the gas token on
Arc, so the difference is what pays for the swap itself. The bot refuses to
start if you get this backwards.
These apply to wallets created AFTER you change them - existing users keep
the caps stored on their record.

$exportComment: Let users export a wallet's private key from the Wallets menu.
It is their key, and a custodial bot you cannot leave is its own problem -
so this defaults ON. But understand the cost: the key is transmitted to
Telegram's servers. The bot deletes its own message after the TTL below,
which keeps it out of scrollback and off a shared screen, but CANNOT
un-send it. Anyone who later compromises that Telegram account may recover
it from backups or a synced device.
Set false to force exports through the operator-local CLI instead:
Note: users who only want their money out should use Withdraw, which sends
to their registered address and never exposes a key.
